fields.Identifying_Data_Chief_Complaint: 28-year-old Asian American female with schizophrenia for routine visit.
fields.Subjective: Patient arrived appearing well-groomed and positive. Reports excellent adherence to aripiprazole 15 mg every morning using daily routine and phone alarm. States feeling very well. No hallucinations for five months. No paranoid thoughts. Sleep excellent at 7-8 hours nightly. Appetite good, weight stable at 118 lbs. Working full-time as kindergarten teaching assistant, loves working with children. Mood 'happy' rated 8/10. Lives with parents which provides cultural and family support. Enjoys baking and sharing treats at work. Attends Asian American mental health support group monthly. One year sober from all substances. Building confidence in career. Considering pursuing teaching credential. No safety concerns, denies SI/HI. (Evaluated by Dr. Susan Wong, MD, Psychiatrist)

Mental_Status_Examination.Appearance: Professionally dressed, excellent grooming, cheerful demeanor

fields.Psychosocial_Context: Lives with parents; employed full-time; one year sober; engaged in support group; considering teaching credential.
Assessment_and_Plan: Excellent medication adherence and symptom control.
fields.Author: Note written by Dr. Susan Wong, MD, Psychiatrist — Children First Mental Health, 4521 School Drive, San Francisco, CA 94102, Phone: (555) 338-7729, Fax: (555) 338-7730
